  • Remove the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} condition
  • Decrease the value of the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened} condition by 1.

Infiltrator's Toolkit (Commercial).name: Infiltrator's Toolkit (Commercial)
Infiltrator's Toolkit (Commercial).description: You need an infiltrator's toolkit to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.2EE4aF4SZpYf0R6H]{Pick Locks} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.cYdz2grcOcRt4jk6]{Disable Devices} (of some types) using the Thievery skill.

Akashic Lens (Commercial).name: Akashic Lens (Commercial)
Akashic Lens (Commercial).description:
Worn as a contact lens, spectacles, or other eyewear, these magically treated lenses project a special data display that uses virtual intelligence and rapid eye tracking to quickly cycle through information until you find what you seek. It grants you a +1 item bonus to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} checks with the skill chosen when you use Database Scan.

Activate—Database Scan 2 (concentrate)

Frequency once per 10 minutes

Effect The lens casts a 1st-rank @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.VynaowCerc7B4yvI]{Akashic Download} that uses the lens instead of a comm unit.

Duo-Enhancers.name: Duo-Enhancers
Duo-Enhancers.description:
This magitech interface is a sophisticated pair of linked bracers, wristbands, gloves, or other garments suitable to the part of a creature's anatomy it uses to wield weapons. One of the paired enhancers includes a screen that displays data about the weapons the wearer is wielding, and the other enhancer contains magical crystals. When you wield a weapon in the hand wearing the display, the weapon's grade (such as tactical) is replicated onto the weapon you're wielding in the hand with the crystal enhancer, but only for purposes of determining the weapon's extra damage dice and tracking trait (which adds an item bonus to attack rolls). Any other properties of the weapon, such as its damage type, ammo, or upgrades, aren't replicated.

The replication functions only if you wear both enhancers, and it ends as soon as you cease wielding a weapon in one of your hands. Consequently, the benefit doesn't apply to thrown attacks or if you're holding a weapon but not wielding it (such as holding in one hand a weapon that requires two hands to wield, or holding a weapon in non-active hands).

Antigrav Harness (Advanced).name: Antigrav Harness (Advanced)
Antigrav Harness (Advanced).description:
This convoluted web of synthweave straps is worn as a fashion statement in some subcultures but has a useful application in extreme environments. You gain a +3 item bonus to Acrobatics checks, and you aren't @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} or clumsy while untethered in zero-g.

Activate—Gravity Alteration 1 (manipulate)

Effect You treat gravity as being two steps higher or lower (for example, zero-g becomes standard gravity, low gravity becomes high gravity, standard gravity becomes extreme gravity, or vice versa). This only affects you, your gear, and your movement and lasts 1 minute.

Gyrojet Pistol.name: Gyrojet Pistol
Gyrojet Pistol.description:
Upgrades: 1

Valued for its stopping power, this pistol sports a large caliber reinforced barrel designed to shoot tiny rockets capable of knocking enemies to the ground.

Smuggler's Grip (Commercial).name: Smuggler's Grip (Commercial)
Smuggler's Grip (Commercial).description: These grips are designed to make it easy to conceal your weapon and are frequently used by detectives, diplomats, and smugglers. The weapon gains the concealable trait.

Medpatch (Ultimate).name: Medpatch (Ultimate)
Medpatch (Ultimate).description:
Activate 1 (manipulate)

Medpatches accelerate the body's natural healing. You regain @Damage[(10d6+27)[healing]] Hit Points and gain a +4 item bonus to saving throws against diseases and poisons for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment-effects.Item.VKSOvJQMwUby4fba]{Effect: Medpatch}

Ivory Chompers.name: Ivory Chompers
Ivory Chompers.description: Tiny pneumatic pistons hidden inside these ornate dental veneers turn them into deadly weapons. Pioneered by the undead media mogul Zo! for celebrities who need to stay armed and fashionable at all times, ivory chompers provide a last-ditch biting attack against attackers. Special designer versions incorporate custom monogrammed imprints, jewels, and metal plating for the ultimate smile.

Retrieval Boosters.name: Retrieval Boosters
Retrieval Boosters.description: When you make a thrown Strike with this weapon, a complex combination of stabilizers and boosters propels it back into your free hand (or other appendage) after the Strike is complete. If your hands are full when the weapon returns, it falls to the ground in your space.

Animated Intelligence (Commercial).name: Animated Intelligence (Commercial)
Animated Intelligence (Commercial).description:
Magical circuitry generates a virtual animated intelligence with a customizable personality and no free will. Rarely, a virtual intelligence might eventually evolve into a true artificial intelligence with self-awareness and free will, usually with the help of powerful magic or a miracle.

Activate—Autoattack 2 (concentrate, manipulate)

Effect You Release the weapon and it glides through the air, fighting on its own against the last enemy you attacked, or the nearest enemy to it if your target has been defeated. At the end of your turn each round, the weapon can Fly up to its fly Speed of 30 feet, and then can either Fly again or Strike one creature within its range or reach.

The weapon has a space of 5 feet, but it doesn't block or impede enemies attempting to move though that space, nor does it benefit from or provide flanking. The animated intelligence is considered a technological creature for the purpose of effects that can target it. The weapon can't move through an enemy's space. The weapon can't use reactions, and its Fly actions don't trigger reactions.

While it's activated, the animated intelligence Strikes with an attack modifier of +18 plus its item bonus to attack rolls. It uses the weapon's normal damage but has a +0 Strength and Dexterity modifier. The weapon's abilities that automatically trigger on a hit or critical hit still function, but the weapon can't be activated or benefit from any of your abilities while animated.

Each round, when the weapon finishes using its actions, attempt a @Check[flat|dc:6]. On a failure, the activation ends. The weapon falls to the ground and can't Autoattack again for 10 minutes.

Hacking Toolkit (Advanced).name: Hacking Toolkit (Advanced)
Hacking Toolkit (Advanced).description: A hacking toolkit is required for Computers checks to Hack computers. You can use a hacking toolkit to access a computer without using a user interface, but this requires physical contact with the computer or contact via an infosphere or a similar linked network. An advanced hacking toolkit adds a +1 item bonus to checks made with it and allows you to access computers at a range of 30 feet, unless you're in a radioactive environment.

Flight Suit.name: Flight Suit
Flight Suit.description:
Upgrades: 0

This comfortable coverall is made from lightweight thermal fabric and often adorned with patches displaying personal insignia, military rank, or even favorite bands or sports teams.

Consecrated Keycaps.name: Consecrated Keycaps
Consecrated Keycaps.description:
These strange keycaps were left as a gift at the memorial of the deceased anacite, Tier-99-Professor.

Activate—Idle Typing 2 (concentrate)

Frequency once per day

Effect You cast a 1st-rank @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.ZBeKxBcUOsXrfMRo]{Implant Data} spell.

Bone Serum.name: Bone Serum
Bone Serum.description:
Activate 1 (manipulate)

This black sludge is distilled from elebrian bone matter and tastes like mint. Upon drinking it, you gain void healing for 1 hour.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment-effects.Item.Crg77XKCHxittH5a]{Effect: Bone Serum}

Autotarget Rifle.name: Autotarget Rifle
Autotarget Rifle.description:
Upgrades: 1

This short, bulky assault rifle incorporates a piston system that allows the user to switch to auto-fire and unload a magazine with one trigger squeeze.

Phase Cutlass.name: Phase Cutlass
Phase Cutlass.description:
Upgrades: 1

A phase cutlass resembles a traditional curved sword until it's powered. Then its blade morphs into a shimmering void, with a tiny star for each life it ended.

Shotalashu Armor.name: Shotalashu Armor
Shotalashu Armor.description:
Upgrades: 1

Flexible but sturdy plates layered together form this traditional armor worn by lashunta cavalry on Castrovel. In times past, a shotalashu cavalry member forged their armor from their mount's shed skin, or more rarely, the skin of a fallen mount, to honor them in future battles. Synthetic imitations are fashionable on some parts of Castrovel.

Painglaive.name: Painglaive
Painglaive.description:
Upgrades: 1

This fierce but deceptively elegant polearm rips apart enemies with a motor-powered fangblade. An adjustable pulse trigger allows the wielder to channel extra pain through the weapon.

Cloaking Device (Tactical).name: Cloaking Device (Tactical)
Cloaking Device (Tactical).description:
This projector bends light around your body.

Activate—Bend Light 1 (manipulate)

Frequency three times per day

Effect You become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} for 1 minute, gaining the effects of a 2nd-rank @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.XXqE1eY3w3z6xJCB]{Invisibility} spell.

Abadarcorp Travel Suit.name: Abadarcorp Travel Suit
Abadarcorp Travel Suit.description:
Upgrades: 2

AbadarCorp executives popularized these armored business suits for boardrooms and battle. Contingent force fields and concealed plating protect the wearer in hostile situations. Higher grades of travel suits offer more protection—along with a designer label.

Dragon Gland (Commercial).name: Dragon Gland (Commercial)
Dragon Gland (Commercial).description:
With cutting edge surgery, you're implanted with an magical gland modified to function for a creature of your ancestry at the back of your throat. Choose your breath weapon's damage type when the organ is biocrafted from the following list: acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You gain the following activation.

Activate—Exhale 1 (manipulate)

Frequency once per 10 minutes

Effect Each creature in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] takes @Damage[(1d6)[untyped]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:15|basic] save. The damage depends on the augmentation's version.

Crystallized Shockshard (Paragon).name: Crystallized Shockshard (Paragon)
Crystallized Shockshard (Paragon).description:
Activate f (concentrate)

Trigger You deal damage to a creature with the weapon.

This electric-blue crystal crackles with energy. When you activate the shockshard, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:38] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 3} for 1 minute. If the target has the tech trait, it instead becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 3}.

Comm Unit.name: Comm Unit
Comm Unit.description:
A personal comm unit is a pocket-sized device that combines a minor portable computer and a cellular communications device, allowing instantaneous wireless communication with other comm units in both audio- and text-based formats at planetary range. A personal comm unit also includes a calculator, a flashlight (emits bright light in a 5-foot radius around you and dim light in the next 5 feet), a browser that accesses any local infospheres, and several entertainment options (including games).

Personal comm units integrated into armor have some voice-activated capabilities; you can communicate with other comm units in audio-based formats and receive responses without needing a free hand, but typing a message, browsing the infosphere, or using an app requires the use of at least one hand.

Comm units that function at longer ranges aren't as portable; they include built-in generators that provide the necessary amount of power and rely on active Drift beacons to communicate. Short-term use of these units can be purchased in most major settlements.

Energy Shielding (Commercial).name: Energy Shielding (Commercial)
Energy Shielding (Commercial).description: This armor is specially treated with insulation to protect against a specific type of damage. You gain resistance 5 to acid, cold, electricity, or fire. The crafter chooses the damage type when creating the upgrade. Multiple energy shielding upgrades can be placed into a suit of armor, but each must provide resistance to a different damage type.

Smuggler's Grip (Tactical).name: Smuggler's Grip (Tactical)
Smuggler's Grip (Tactical).description:
These grips are designed to make it easy to conceal your weapon and are frequently used by detectives, diplomats, and smugglers. The weapon gains the concealable trait.

A tactical smuggler's grip is a hybrid item with the extradimensional, magical, and tech traits. You can pull the weapon into an extradimensional space as an Interact action, leaving behind a palm-sized disk and granting you a +4 item bonus to your Stealth check to Conceal the Object. You can't draw the weapon while it's in the extradimensional space, but you can use an Interact action to retrieve it.

Radiation Sweeper.name: Radiation Sweeper
Radiation Sweeper.description: This handheld device is capable of detecting radiation within 120 feet. When you Interact with the device, it emits pings in different tones to indicate the presence of low, medium, high, or severe radiation. It also displays the areas of radiation on a simple screen. It automatically updates the data as you move until the beginning of your next turn. After that, you must Interact with the device again to conduct another sweep. A radiation sweeper can't detect radiation through 3 feet of dirt or wood, 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, a thin sheet of lead, or any force field. A radiation sweeper is immune to the effects of radiation.

Truesight Sight (Commercial).name: Truesight Sight (Commercial)
Truesight Sight (Commercial).description:
Lenses are made of crystals specially infused with spells as they're cut, then installed into autofocusing sights. You gain a +1 item bonus to visual Perception checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} when looking through the scope.

Activate—See the Truth 2 (manipulate)

Frequency once per day

Effect For the next 10 minutes, while looking through the scope, you can see @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} creatures as though they weren't invisible. Additionally, you can see incorporeal creatures, like ghosts, hiding within 10 feet of an object's surface.

Undermounted Grenade Launcher (Advanced).name: Undermounted Grenade Launcher (Advanced)
Undermounted Grenade Launcher (Advanced).description:
This bracket-mounted grenade launcher is most often advertised for use with fragmentation grenades, but can efficiently deploy utility ordinances, such as smoke grenades and flashbangs. Unlike a dedicated grenade launcher, it takes 10 minutes to load an undermounted grenade launcher, reassembling the grenade into ammunition by mounting it on a specialized reusable firing cap. Once a grenade has been loaded, it can only be safely removed by disassembling the entire grenade, destroying it in the process.

This grenade launcher has a range of 40 feet and the tracking +2 trait.

Activate—Launch 1 (attack)

Effect You launch the loaded grenade.

Congealer Incense (Tactical).name: Congealer Incense (Tactical)
Congealer Incense (Tactical).description:
This delicate strand of incense is packaged in a hand-blown glass vial and crafted from petrified wood of centuries-old Eoxian trees, peppered with a tincture of elebrian ashes and enchanted sand from the Deminas Ocean. When exposed to air by breaking the container, the incense bursts into cloying, lavender-scented smoke that sticks to incorporeal creatures and stabilizes their form.

Activate—Break Open 1 (manipulate) Duration 1 minute

Effect Purple smoke billows out from the incense in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5]. At the start of each round, this emanation increases by 5 feet (to a maximum of 30 feet). The creature who activated the congealer incense can drop it in a space as a free action, or can continue to carry it with them in 1 hand, thus moving the center of the emanation on that creature's turn. Any incorporeal creature who enters this area or begins their turn inside of it must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:27] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success Treat the incorporeal creature's resistances as 4 lower.

Failure As success, but the creature loses all immunity to precision damage and treats its resistances as 8 lower.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Merciful.name: 仁慈 Merciful
Merciful.description:
Merciful weapons are limned in a green glow recognized around the galaxy. A merciful weapon has the nonlethal trait and can't be used to make a lethal attack. Any persistent damage the weapon would deal is negated.

On a critical hit, a merciful weapon causes the target to become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} with the weapon's wielder for 1 minute, as the target is bombarded with feelings of guilt and remorse. This is a mental effect. If you have access to the weapon's critical specialization effect, you choose whether to use it or the merciful effect.

Incendiary Grenade (Superior).name: Incendiary Grenade (Superior)
Incendiary Grenade (Superior).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical 10 feet

These grenades use an ignited chemical to explode in a blast of intense heat and deal 8d8 fire damage with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save (@Template[burst|distance:15]). If you have access to the critical specialization effect of grenades, creatures who critically fail their saving throw against incendiary grenades are knocked away from the grenade.

Iovan Ammunition.name: Iovan Ammunition
Iovan Ammunition.description:
Ammunition projectile

Harvested from the Diaspora by Ulrikka Clanholdings, these rocks contain charged particles from the Astral Plane. When forged in a process that is part flint-knapping and part occult communion, skilled artificers can turn shards of these rocks into ammunition capable of harming incorporeal beings. Each piece of Iovan ammunition functions as though the weapon firing it had the ghost killer weapon upgrade. The brittle ammunition deals 0 damage against corporeal targets, as it breaks apart upon impact. Iovan ammunition deals half damage (rounded down) to an incorporeal creature possessing a living creature, making it popular with exorcists throughout the galaxy.

Kyokor Plating.name: Kyokor Plating
Kyokor Plating.description:
Upgrade: 1

This armor incorporates a full-coverage polycarbon suit studded with ports and sockets and layered with plates from Daimalko's infamous colossi. Kyokor plating comes with boots, gloves, and a helmet with a clear visor.

Hypopen (Elite).name: Hypopen (Elite)
Hypopen (Elite).description:
This tiny cylinder contains specialized nanites that either

  • Remove the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} condition
  • Decrease the value of the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened} condition by 1.
  • Attempt a counteract check against one poison or disease afflicting the target. Use your @Check[medicine] modifier for the counteract modifier and a counteract rank of 6.
  • Attempt a flat check to end persistent poison or bleed damage. As this is particularly effective assistance, the DC of the flat check is reduced from 15 to 10 for this check.

  • Allow you to become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} for 1 minute. You can use the extra action to Step, Stride, or Strike.

    @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment-effects.Item.NqP5f6IChp8XNCrJ]{Effect: Hypopen (Quickened)}

Radiation Buffer (Advanced).name: Radiation Buffer (Advanced)
Radiation Buffer (Advanced).description: A layer of treated lead lines your armor with integrated radiation sensors networked into your comm unit. While you're wearing armor with this upgrade, you gain resistance 8 to poison damage from radiation effects and a +3 status bonus to Fortitude saves against radiation.

Crossbolter.name: Crossbolter
Crossbolter.description:
Upgrades: 1

This high-tech crossbow uses a series of pistons to propel bolts along its barrel. These quiet-firing semiautomatic weapons are popular in high-density settlements where gunfire might cause a panic.

Flaming Module (Commercial).name: Flaming Module (Commercial)
Flaming Module (Commercial).description: This module overheats the weapon's striking edge and ammunition. The weapon deals an additional 1d6 fire damage on a successful Strike, plus 1d10 persistent fire damage on a critical hit.

Incendiary Grenade (Advanced).name: Incendiary Grenade (Advanced)
Incendiary Grenade (Advanced).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical 5 feet

These grenades use an ignited chemical to explode in a blast of intense heat and deal 3d8 fire damage with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save (@Template[burst|distance:10]). If you have access to the critical specialization effect of grenades, creatures who critically fail their saving throw against incendiary grenades are knocked away from the grenade.

Maratan Headache (Ultimate).name: Maratan Headache (Ultimate)
Maratan Headache (Ultimate).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{sickened 2}

Maratan headaches are made from brittle hollow gourds filled with a putrid sludge, dealing 12d8 nonlethal poison damage in a @Template[burst|distance:20] with a @Check[fortitude|against:class|basic] save.

Ultralight Wings (Advanced).name: Ultralight Wings (Advanced)
Ultralight Wings (Advanced).description: Foldable wings are attached to your shoulder blades or a similar location on your body that allows them to lift your weight and assist your flying movements. You can deploy the wings with an Interact action, but you can't deploy or use them while you're wearing more than light armor. While the wings are deployed and you're not encumbered, you gain a fly Speed of 40 feet and a +2 item bonus to Acrobatics checks to Maneuver in Flight.

Ultralight Wings (Tactical).name: Ultralight Wings (Tactical)
Ultralight Wings (Tactical).description: Foldable wings are attached to your shoulder blades or a similar location on your body that allows them to lift your weight and assist your flying movements. You can deploy the wings with an Interact action, but you can't deploy or use them while you're wearing more than light armor. While the wings are deployed and you're not encumbered, you gain a fly Speed of 30 feet and a +1 item bonus to Acrobatics checks to Maneuver in Flight.

Filtered Rebreather (Elite).name: Filtered Rebreather (Elite)
Filtered Rebreather (Elite).description:
This armor upgrade supplements your armor's environmental protections and filters out common contaminants, including protection from most toxic atmospheres.

You gain a +4 status bonus to Fortitude saves against inhaled effects.

Striking Crystal (Tactical).name: Striking Crystal (Tactical)
Striking Crystal (Tactical).description: This gem contains the condensed matter of a star and increases the weapon damage dice of a solar weapon or flare to three instead of one.

Holy Module (Commercial).name: Holy Module (Commercial)
Holy Module (Commercial).description: This upgrade is often adorned with a religious symbol and channels celestial power. It deals an additional 1d4 spirit damage, or 3d4 spirit against an unholy target. If you are unholy, you are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} while carrying or wielding this weapon. On a critical hit, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} if unholy) until the end of your next turn unless it succeeds at a @Check[fortitude|dc:28] save.

Rotating Pistol.name: Rotating Pistol
Rotating Pistol.description:
Upgrades: 1

A time-tested design favored by traditional gunslingers, the multiple barrels on this pistol mechanically rotate while firing, making it less likely to malfunction.

Hygiene Kit.name: Hygiene Kit
Hygiene Kit.description: This kit contains all the tools and products needed to practice good grooming and hygiene. Kits designed for specific creatures include supplies (such as scale-care products for vesk or antennae grooming oil for lashuntas or shirrens) that are of less use to other creatures.

Necrolung (Tactical).name: Necrolung (Tactical)
Necrolung (Tactical).description:
Spongy black webbing radiates from a grayish purple mass of necrotized flesh that pulses when it's exposed to air. Specially grown in vats from waste produced in the necrograft manufacturing process, necrolungs allow living creatures to breathe efficiently and safely on Eox, becoming effectively immune to the toxins in its atmosphere for an additional 24 hours per level, and you gain a +1 item bonus to saves against diseases and poisons; this bonus increases to +2 if the effect is inhaled.

Installing a necrolung takes one minute and requires no skill checks. When installed, a necrolung slithers down your throat (or appropriate alien anatomy) and integrates into your respiratory system. Most users notice a harmless pallor and rotting smell while using the tech.

Artillery Laser.name: Artillery Laser
Artillery Laser.description:
Upgrades: 1

The fast-firing and continuous beams of this laser cannon converge on the target to create enough heat to melt a steel door into slag.

Shrink Spell Amp.name: Shrink Spell Amp
Shrink Spell Amp.description:
Known on most worlds as "spell amps," spell ampoules are ready-to-use magic injections that confer the benefits of a spell of the specified rank.

Activate—Inject 1 (manipulate)

Effect 2nd-rank @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.PjhUmyKnq6K5uDby]{Shrink} spell

Hideaway Limb (Tactical).name: Hideaway Limb (Tactical)
Hideaway Limb (Tactical).description:
You can conceal items inside a hidden compartment in one of your limbs (such as an arm, leg, or tail) and protect them with a passcode. The compartment holds items of negligible Bulk and items of light Bulk that are small enough to fit in your limb or can otherwise be folded or contracted to fit. A hideaway in a leg (in a human) can typically hold more than one in an arm, and your size also adjusts the capacity at the GM's discretion.

When the compartment is closed, the seam in your limb is difficult to detect. You gain a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks to Conceal an Object in a hideaway limb. Though this compartment can foil a cursory pat down, many security checkpoints include a scan to detect such devices.

Activate—Draw 1 (manipulate)

Effect You Interact to draw an item @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} in your hideaway limb.

When you use Draw, if the item can be activated or otherwise used with a single action, you can use the item as part of the same action. For example, you Interact to draw a weapon, then Strike with that weapon.

Gluon Crystal (Commercial).name: Gluon Crystal (Commercial)
Gluon Crystal (Commercial).description: A gluon crystal harnesses entropy to cause serious lasting wounds. Your solar weapon or solar flare deals an extra @Damage[1d4[persistent,bleed]] damage. On a critical hit, the target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} until it recovers from the persistent bleed damage.

Slough-Do (Tactical).name: Slough-Do (Tactical)
Slough-Do (Tactical).description:
This wad of green slime looks like a mass of jelly, but when worn on the head, you can Interact to style it into hair-do-like fashions. While worn, rivulets of green liquid slowly dribble into your ears and down your forehead and neck, making it uncomfortable to use long-term.

Slough-do grants you a +2 item bonus to Occultism checks, and allows you to cast the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.h7IhypLwItepwhou]{Adhere} cantrip as an occult innate spell.

Additionally, you can activate the tactical slough-do.

Activate—Slough-Do Swirl Cast a Spell

Frequency once per day

Effect The slough-do swirls rapidly, causing occult energy to pour from it. You cast a 4th-rank @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.jyrxfWeeCsrkhNit]{Eldritch Wrath} spell.

Pow! Ammunition.name: Pow! Ammunition
Pow! Ammunition.description:
Ammunition projectile

Invented by Zo! Media prop designers, this special ammunition is designed to explode in a dramatic fanfare fit for a media production without sacrificing any lethality. A weapon loaded with POW! ammunition automatically attempts to [[/act feint]] targets within its first range increment. Targets of your Feint are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} against ranged attacks in your first range increment rather than melee attacks, depending on the result of the save.

Dermal Plating (Commercial).name: Dermal Plating (Commercial)
Dermal Plating (Commercial).description: Hardened plates protect your skin and help you deflect physical blows. A limb with dermal plating functions as a shield. You can Raise a Shield with your limb, giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, but it doesn't require a hand to use. You can use the Shield Block reaction with your limb. Dermal plating has Hardness 5. After you use Shield Block, your dermal plating becomes damaged and can't be used for 24 hours as the nanites repair the durable plates.

Talon.name: Talon
Talon.description:
Upgrades: 1

This stylized knife tapers into a curved and serrated blade. When powered, it spews a highly corrosive acid inspired by the armor-melting talons evolved by several species of the Swarm.

Shielding Skin (Tactical).name: Shielding Skin (Tactical)
Shielding Skin (Tactical).description: Your skin is biologically engineered to protect your entire body, including your brain, from harmful effects. You gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws.

Plasma Sword.name: Plasma Sword
Plasma Sword.description:
Upgrades: 1

This sword generates a focused beam of plasma around a localized force field powered by a generator in the sword's hilt. The beam only blazes to life when the sword is powered.

Truesight Sight (Tactical).name: Truesight Sight (Tactical)
Truesight Sight (Tactical).description:
Lenses are made of crystals specially infused with spells as they're cut, then installed into autofocusing sights. You gain a +2 item bonus to visual Perception checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} when looking through the scope.

Activate—See the Truth 2 (manipulate)

Frequency once per day

Effect For the next 10 minutes, while looking through the scope, you can see @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} creatures as though they weren't invisible. Additionally, you can see incorporeal creatures, like ghosts, hiding within 10 feet of an object's surface.

When you activate See the Truth, you see the truth of the world. The GM rolls a secret counteract check with a [[/r 1d20+20 #Counteract]]{+20} counteract modifier and a counteract rank of 7 against any illusion, morph, or polymorph effect in the area, but only for the purpose of determining whether you see through it, not to end the spell or effect.

Reality Distorter.name: Reality Distorter
Reality Distorter.description:
This device emits ripples of energy creating small distortions within reality around your foes.

Activate—Distort! r

Frequency once per 10 minutes

Trigger A creature moves adjacent to you

Effect Your armor unleashes a bubble of distorted reality keyed to the quantum signature of the triggering creature in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] centered on you that lasts 1 minute. The triggering creature treats the area as difficult terrain.

Aeon Stone (Uplifting).name: Aeon Stone (Uplifting)
Aeon Stone (Uplifting).description:
Staple items within the Azlanti Star Empire, these gemstones are popular status symbols for adventurers and wealthy collectors. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to build the Universe in ancient times.

When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head (or another visible part of your anatomy if you don't have a head) instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an aeon stone with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.enA7BxAjBb7ns1iF]{Suppressed} until you return it to orbit your head again.

There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into specific items, such as weapons with the aeon trait or antique magical devices called wayfinders.

This deep-blue prism with cloudy inclusions is light as a feather. Its resonant power gives you a +1 item bonus to Acrobatics checks.

Activate—Fly Free 1 (concentrate)

Frequency once per day

Effect You gain a fly Speed for 1 minute equal to your Speed or 20 feet, whichever is greater.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment-effects.Item.xZ5XMY55S42aGyjg]{Effect: Aeon Stone (Uplifting)}

Hypopen (Superior).name: Hypopen (Superior)
Hypopen (Superior).description:
This tiny cylinder contains specialized nanites that either

  • Remove the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} condition
  • Decrease the value of the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened} condition by 1.
  • Attempt a counteract check against one poison or disease afflicting the target. Use your @Check[medicine] modifier for the counteract modifier and a counteract rank of 5.
  • Attempt a flat check to end persistent poison or bleed damage. As this is particularly effective assistance, the DC of the flat check is reduced from 15 to 10 for this check.

Maratan Headache (Elite).name: Maratan Headache (Elite)
Maratan Headache (Elite).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{sickened 2}

Maratan headaches are made from brittle hollow gourds filled with a putrid sludge, dealing 10d8 nonlethal poison damage in a @Template[burst|distance:15] with a @Check[fortitude|against:class|basic] save.

Maker's App.name: Maker's App
Maker's App.description: This comm unit app contains formulas for Crafting the common items in this section.

Lock (Advanced).name: Lock (Advanced)
Lock (Advanced).description: Picking an advanced lock requires four successful [[/act pick-a-lock dc=25]]{DC 25 Thievery} checks.

Null Space Chamber (Superior).name: Null Space Chamber (Superior)
Null Space Chamber (Superior).description:
This sleek black device attaches to a backpack, purse, or key ring. When you press a button on the side of the device, it creates a circular portal to an extradimensional pocket space. The space's capacity depends on the device's type.

You can Interact with the null space chamber to stow items in it or remove them. The null space chamber can be opened and closed only from the outside. When a null space chamber is opened in an atmosphere with air, it fills with 10 minutes' worth of air for every Bulk left in its capacity. An item inside the null space chamber provides no benefits unless it's retrieved first. Connecting the null space chamber to a computer causes it to display a readout of all items stored within it.

Capacity 150 Bulk

Diva's Microphone (Commercial).name: Diva's Microphone (Commercial)
Diva's Microphone (Commercial).description:
This ostentatious crystal microphone is embellished with glimmering jewels and delicate lights that pulse in time to music. It functions as both a microphone and speaker, amplifying your voice loud enough for you to be heard clearly by all listeners up to 500 feet away, even if other ambient noise would otherwise block the sound. This doesn't increase the range or area of other auditory or linguistic effects, and physical barriers such as walls still block or muffle your voice as normal.

The diva's microphone grants a +1 item bonus to Performance checks attempted with it.

Activate—Grand Finale 2 (auditory)

Frequency once per 10 minutes

Effect Your song reaches an eardrum-bursting climax as you cast a 1st-rank @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.QpP30fcEl4NGjHwo]{Sonic Scream} originating from your space (DC 18).

Machine Mind Serum (Tactical).name: Machine Mind Serum (Tactical)
Machine Mind Serum (Tactical).description:
Activate 1 (manipulate)

When you inject this serum, magitech nanites infuse your body with machine-like circuitry that pulses and glows beneath your skin. For 10 minutes, you gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against mental effects. You also gain resistance 10 to mental damage. Due to your more machine-like nature, you gain weakness 10 to electricity.

Sniper's Scope (Tactical).name: Sniper's Scope (Tactical)
Sniper's Scope (Tactical).description:
A telescopic sighting attachment helps view distant targets.

The scope grants you a +1 item bonus to visual Perception checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} creatures through the scope.

Activate—Adjust Sight 1 (manipulate)

Effect Increase the range increment of the weapon the scope is attached to by 10 feet. If the weapon has a base range increment of 50 feet or more, increase the range of the weapon by 20 feet instead. This lasts until the beginning of your next turn or until you're no longer wielding the weapon this scope is attached to, whichever comes first. This does not increase the size of area attacks.

Smoke Grenade (Elite).name: Smoke Grenade (Elite)
Smoke Grenade (Elite).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

This grenade unleashes a cloud of concealing smoke in a @Template[burst|distance:40]. Creatures within the area have the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} condition, and all other creatures are concealed to them. The smoke lasts for 1 minute or until dissipated by a strong wind or similar effect.

Replica Zo! Microphone.name: Replica Zo! Microphone
Replica Zo! Microphone.description:
Upgrades 1

This ornate replica of the undead superstar's iconic microphone is an officially licensed Zo! Media product. Zo!'s microphone is rumored to be a bone scepter upgraded with the latest technologies to amplify Zo!'s voice for blasting enemies or unruly fans. The replica version possesses similar capabilities and can be used as a professional microphone or a weapon.

Mobile Bulwark.name: Mobile Bulwark
Mobile Bulwark.description: This bulky shield is advertised as the ultimate protection in battle for those strong enough to hoist them. Mobile bulwarks create an iconic rectangular silhouette and are popular with heavily armored mercenaries and soldiers, notably among the Veskarium military and some orders of Hellknights. A mobile bulwark is often constructed of carbon steel, with a transparent aluminum view port.

Radiation Buffer (Tactical).name: Radiation Buffer (Tactical)
Radiation Buffer (Tactical).description: A layer of treated lead lines your armor with integrated radiation sensors networked into your comm unit. While you're wearing armor with this upgrade, you gain resistance 4 to poison damage from radiation effects and a +2 status bonus to Fortitude saves against radiation.

Aeon Stone (Navigating).name: Aeon Stone (Navigating)
Aeon Stone (Navigating).description:
Staple items within the Azlanti Star Empire, these gemstones are popular status symbols for adventurers and wealthy collectors. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to build the Universe in ancient times.

When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head (or another visible part of your anatomy if you don't have a head) instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an aeon stone with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.enA7BxAjBb7ns1iF]{Suppressed} until you return it to orbit your head again.

There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into specific items, such as weapons with the aeon trait or antique magical devices called wayfinders.

This deep purple helicoid prism guides your innate sense of direction in even the emptiest reaches of space. You gain a +2 item bonus to Piloting checks to Navigate. The resonant power allows you to cast @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.3VucgRc8FRPGy9NI]{Seek the Stars} as a divine innate cantrip.

Activate—Navigate 1 (concentrate)

Frequency once per day

Effect You concentrate on a Drift beacon you've visited before and mentally chart a course for that location. You immediately know which direction to travel to reach your destination, and your journey takes 1 day less if you're piloting the ship.

Maratan Headache (Paragon).name: Maratan Headache (Paragon)
Maratan Headache (Paragon).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{sickened 3}

Maratan headaches are made from brittle hollow gourds filled with a putrid sludge, dealing 14d8 nonlethal poison damage in a @Template[burst|distance:20] with a @Check[fortitude|against:class|basic] save.

Darkvision Capacitors (Tactical).name: Darkvision Capacitors (Tactical)
Darkvision Capacitors (Tactical).description:
These replacement cybernetic eyes grant you darkvision. Darkvision capacitors operate by emitting low-powered ultraviolet lasers that receptors in the capacitors can detect. These lasers are visible to creatures that can see on the ultraviolet light spectrum.

These upgraded capacitors include a wide-spectrum ocular implant that allows you to see infrared and ultraviolet light, including the lasers from other darkvision capacitors. You gain darkvision and a +2 item bonus to Perception checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} if they involve sight.

Dragon Gland (Advanced).name: Dragon Gland (Advanced)
Dragon Gland (Advanced).description:
With cutting edge surgery, you're implanted with an magical gland modified to function for a creature of your ancestry at the back of your throat. Choose your breath weapon's damage type when the organ is biocrafted from the following list: acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You gain the following activation.

Activate—Exhale 1 (manipulate)

Frequency once per 10 minutes

Effect Each creature in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] takes @Damage[(4d6)[untyped]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:23|basic] save. The damage depends on the augmentation's version.

Wheelchair.name: 轮椅 Wheelchair
Wheelchair.description: Wheelchairs come in a variety of sizes and styles to suit countless alien body types, including hoverchairs. Automated mechanisms allow wheelchairs to traverse stairs, ladders, and uneven ground without any additional difficulty (difficult terrain still affects your Speed while using a wheelchair).

Congealer Incense (Advanced).name: Congealer Incense (Advanced)
Congealer Incense (Advanced).description:
This delicate strand of incense is packaged in a hand-blown glass vial and crafted from petrified wood of centuries-old Eoxian trees, peppered with a tincture of elebrian ashes and enchanted sand from the Deminas Ocean. When exposed to air by breaking the container, the incense bursts into cloying, lavender-scented smoke that sticks to incorporeal creatures and stabilizes their form.

Activate—Break Open 1 (manipulate) Duration 1 minute

Effect Purple smoke billows out from the incense in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5]. At the start of each round, this emanation increases by 5 feet (to a maximum of 30 feet). The creature who activated the congealer incense can drop it in a space as a free action, or can continue to carry it with them in 1 hand, thus moving the center of the emanation on that creature's turn. Any incorporeal creature who enters this area or begins their turn inside of it must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:34] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success Treat the incorporeal creature's resistances as 4 lower.

Failure As success, but the creature loses all immunity to precision damage and treats its resistances as 8 lower.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Programmer's Plushie (Advanced).name: Programmer's Plushie (Advanced)
Programmer's Plushie (Advanced).description:
These miniatures come in many shapes, often resembling a programmer's personal avatar or popular media character, such as Captain Concierge or Zo!. The plush helps organize your thoughts; you gain a +3 item bonus to Computers checks.

Activate—Secret Password 1 (auditory)

Effect You whisper a secret passcode known only to yourself and your programmer's plushie, securing a computer, container, door, or lock from intrusion as a 7th-rank @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.Azoh0BSoCASrA1lr]{Lock} with a DC of 35. Any creature can bypass this lock using the passcode while touching the target with the programmer's plushie.

Thermal Dynafan.name: Thermal Dynafan
Thermal Dynafan.description:
Upgrades: 1

This warfan is a modified handheld environmental comfort device that changes the pressure in the air to create a frigid chill or a brilliant plume of fire.

Singing Coil.name: Singing Coil
Singing Coil.description:
Upgrades: 1

Resembling a resonant transformer circuit, this weaponized stringed instrument produces sound by using high-frequency alternating currents to cause air molecules to vibrate.

Riot Shield.name: Riot Shield
Riot Shield.description: Riot shields are constructed from sheets of transparent aluminum reinforced with nanocarbon panels layered over polymers or resins. These sleek but sturdy shields are designed to protect most of the wielder's body when raised while allowing them to march in tight tactical formations. Sometimes they're transparent to allow inexperienced wielders to see the other side while the shield is raised, but most experienced warriors have no problem seeing around these shields, and the transparent material quickly becomes too scuffed to see through after a few hits.

Reaction Breacher.name: Reaction Breacher
Reaction Breacher.description:
Upgrades: 1

This portable reaction cannon launches flak from miniature cluster missiles that rip through a fortified structure and anyone unfortunate enough to be inside.

Silencer (Commercial).name: Silencer (Commercial)
Silencer (Commercial).description: A cylinder is filled with internal sound baffles to reduce noise. The weapon doesn't make a loud noise when fired. However, creatures hit by the ammunition might still cry out in pain.

Crystallized Shockshard (Ultimate).name: Crystallized Shockshard (Ultimate)
Crystallized Shockshard (Ultimate).description:
Activate f (concentrate)

Trigger You deal damage to a creature with the weapon.

This electric-blue crystal crackles with energy. When you activate the shockshard, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:34] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 3} for 1 minute. If the target has the tech trait, it instead becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 3}.

Zero Cannon.name: Zero Cannon
Zero Cannon.description:
Upgrades: 1

This triple-barreled cannon's generator churns rapidly, circulating coolant into a blast of endothermic energy. While powered, frost rimes the barrel and stock of this weapon, causing experienced wielders to wear thermal gloves.

Flaming Module (Tactical).name: Flaming Module (Tactical)
Flaming Module (Tactical).description:
This module overheats the weapon's striking edge and ammunition. The weapon deals an additional 1d6 fire damage on a successful Strike, plus 2d10 persistent fire damage on a critical hit.

Fire damage dealt by this weapon (including the persistent fire damage) ignores the target's fire resistance.

Electromag Grenade (Advanced).name: Electromag Grenade (Advanced)
Electromag Grenade (Advanced).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}

These grenades discharge a shocking field that deals 3d8 electricity damage in a @Template[burst|distance:10] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save. If you have access to the critical specialization effect of grenades, tech creatures and unattended tech items that critically fail their saving throw against electromag grenades become glitching.

Autograppler (Tactical).name: Autograppler (Tactical)
Autograppler (Tactical).description:
You can attach this anchoring device to a cable line with an Interact action. Clamps automatically slide from the base of the autograppler along the attached cable, allowing it to be climbed like a knotted rope. You can throw the autograppler to attach it to a solid surface, attempting a ranged attack roll using your simple weapon proficiency against a DC depending on the target, typically at least DC 20. This attack has the secret trait. On a success, your autograppler has a firm hold, but on a critical failure, the grappler seems like it will hold but detaches when you're partway up.

A tactical autograppler has a sensor to detect whether it has a firm hold, causing it to automatically retract if you critically fail your ranged attack to attach it.

Striking Crystal (Advanced).name: Striking Crystal (Advanced)
Striking Crystal (Advanced).description: This gem contains the condensed matter of a star and increases the weapon damage dice of a solar weapon or flare to four instead of one.

Venom Spur.name: Venom Spur
Venom Spur.description: A retractable spur attached to one of your appendages delivers a poisonous sting. You gain a venom spur unarmed attack that deals 1d6 piercing plus 1d4 persistent poison damage. Your venom spur is in the brawling group and has the finesse, poison, and unarmed traits.

Shock Module (Tactical).name: Shock Module (Tactical)
Shock Module (Tactical).description:
This circuit safely courses electricity through your weapon. It deals an extra 1d6 electricity damage. On a critical hit, this increases to 2d10 electricity damage, and electricity arcs out to deal an equal amount of electricity damage to up to two other creatures of your choice within 10 feet of the target.

Electricity damage dealt by this weapon ignores the target's electricity resistance (and the other creatures' on a critical hit).

Spell Chip (5th-Rank Spell).name: Spell Chip (5th-Rank Spell)
Spell Chip (5th-Rank Spell).description:
Usage installed in computer or comm unit;

These chips can be installed in comm units and other computers to allow the device to cast spells. Each device can have only one spell chip. Casting a spell from a spell chip requires holding the computer or comm unit in one hand and activating it with a Cast a Spell activity using the normal number of actions for that spell. The spell must appear on your spell list. Because you're the one Casting the Spell, use your spell attack modifier and spell DC. The spell must be of your spellcasting tradition. If the spell has a locus, you must still have that locus to cast the spell from a spell chip. Spell chips can't contain cantrips or rituals. The traits for this item vary based on the spell it contains.

In addition to the charge expended to Cast the Spell, the delicate components that make up the core lattice of a spell chip can be destroyed if used in quick succession. Attempting to use a spell chip more than once per day requires overclocking it. Cast the Spell again, then roll a @Check[flat|dc:10]. On a success, the spell chip is broken. On a failure, the spell chip is destroyed. If anyone tries to overclock a spell chip that's already been overclocked that day, the spell chip is automatically destroyed (even if it's been repaired) and no spell is cast.

Activate Cast a Spell

Frequency once per day, plus overclock

Effect You Cast the Spell at the indicated rank.

Electromag Grenade (Superior).name: Electromag Grenade (Superior)
Electromag Grenade (Superior).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}

These grenades discharge a shocking field that deals 8d8 electricity damage in a @Template[burst|distance:15] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save. If you have access to the critical specialization effect of grenades, tech creatures and unattended tech items that critically fail their saving throw against electromag grenades become glitching.

Psychoactive Eyes (Tactical).name: Psychoactive Eyes (Tactical)
Psychoactive Eyes (Tactical).description:
Your irises are injected with psychoactive dyes that cause a signature kaleidoscopic effect, or your eyes have been completely replaced with delicate magitech devices. You can activate these dyes to enthrall those you look upon with varying effects depending on the item's type.

Activate—Gaze 2 (concentrate, mental, visual)

Frequency once per day

Effect You focus your eyes on a creature that can see you within 30 feet. The creature attempts a @Check[will|dc:20] saving throw with the following results.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature is fascinated and becomes helpful for as long as it can see you. Taking a hostile action against the creature ends the effect.

Musical Instrument (Handheld, Commercial.name: Musical Instrument (Handheld, Commercial)
Musical Instrument (Handheld, Commercial.description: All but the most traditional instruments use modern technology to amplify their sound, provide musical accompaniment, or connect wirelessly to other instruments. Handheld instruments include autohorns, guitars, psychoactive wave plates, digital synth decks, holoflutes, and more. The GM might rule that an especially large handheld instrument has greater Bulk. Some instruments have the tech trait.

Marrow Paste (Superior).name: Marrow Paste (Superior)
Marrow Paste (Superior).description: Developed and patented by Eoxian medical conglomerate Nulife, this magenta tincture's metallic odor is reminiscent of fresh blood. When applied to the body of a creature that can benefit from void healing, marrow paste grants fast healing 10 for 1 minute.

Frag Grenade (Advanced).name: Frag Grenade (Advanced)
Frag Grenade (Advanced).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @Damage[1d6[bleed]]

These fragmentary grenades explode in a cloud of shrapnel and deal 3d8 piercing damage in a @Template[burst|distance:10] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save.

Energy Shielding (Tactical).name: Energy Shielding (Tactical)
Energy Shielding (Tactical).description: This armor is specially treated with insulation to protect against a specific type of damage. You gain resistance 10 to acid, cold, electricity, or fire. The crafter chooses the damage type when creating the upgrade. Multiple energy shielding upgrades can be placed into a suit of armor, but each must provide resistance to a different damage type.

Flash Grenade (Ultimate).name: Flash Grenade (Ultimate)
Flash Grenade (Ultimate).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical dazzled for 2 rounds

A flash grenade unleashes a blast of bright light that requires creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:5] to attempt a @Check[fortitude|against:class] save. On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Spell Gem (2nd-Rank Spell).name: Spell Gem (2nd-Rank Spell)
Spell Gem (2nd-Rank Spell).description:
These latticed gemstones are suspended in a silicon casing and encoded with the magical script necessary to cast a single, specific spell. Casting a spell from a spell gem requires holding the gem in one hand and activating it with a Cast a Spell activity using the normal number of actions for that spell. The spell must appear on your spell list. Because you're the one Casting the Spell, use your spell attack modifier and spell DC. The spell must be of your spellcasting tradition. The spell in a spell gem can be cast only once, and the spell gem is destroyed as part of the casting. Spell gems can't contain cantrips or rituals. The traits for this item vary based on the spell it contains.

Activate Cast a Spell

Effect You Cast the Spell at the indicated rank.

Radiation Buffer (Commercial).name: Radiation Buffer (Commercial)
Radiation Buffer (Commercial).description: A layer of treated lead lines your armor with integrated radiation sensors networked into your comm unit. While you're wearing armor with this upgrade, you gain resistance 2 to poison damage from radiation effects and a +1 status bonus to Fortitude saves against radiation.

Dragonsight Flashlight.name: Dragonsight Flashlight
Dragonsight Flashlight.description:
The latest innovation from Frozen Trove Labs is a tactical flashlight with special filters which can be activated to locate hidden objects.

Activate—Enhancement Filter 1 (manipulate)

Frequency once per day

Effect All creatures gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to locate hidden things in the area of the dragonsight flashlight's light, such as hidden traps and secret doors, for 30 minutes.

Field Scientist's Toolkit (Commercial).name: Field Scientist's Toolkit (Commercial)
Field Scientist's Toolkit (Commercial).description: A field scientist toolkit contains a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment.Item.btcbYUK4UqgQ699i]{Chemalyzer}, microscope, recording tools, and specimen bags.

Maker's Toolkit (Tactical).name: Maker's Toolkit (Tactical)
Maker's Toolkit (Tactical).description: You need a maker's toolkit to create items from UPBs with the Craft skill. A tactical maker's toolkit gives you a +1 item bonus to the check.

Degradation Grenade (Advanced).name: Degradation Grenade (Advanced)
Degradation Grenade (Advanced).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @Damage[1d6[persistent,acid]] damage

Degradation grenades create violent blasts of magic that rip matter into different realities, leaving behind blackened hunks of matter that dissipate into nothingness. When the grenade explodes, it deals 3d8 acid damage in a @Template[burst|distance:10] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save.

Hardlight Series.name: Hardlight Series
Hardlight Series.description:
Upgrades: 1

A projector fastened to a belt or lapel encases the wearer's body in this shimmering hardlight shell. Military-issue hardlight armor follows a predictable standard, but private users enjoy a range of customizable fashion options.

Freebooter Armor.name: Freebooter Armor
Freebooter Armor.description:
Upgrades: 1

Freebooter armor is named for the pirates and outlaws who often wear these outfits. Freebooter armor typically consists of a breastplate worn under a jacket or flight suit, heavy boots and gloves, a helmet, and hidden straps and holsters for concealing weapons.

Musical Instrument (Heavy, Commercial).name: Musical Instrument (Heavy, Commercial)
Musical Instrument (Heavy, Commercial).description: All but the most traditional instruments use modern technology to amplify their sound, provide musical accompaniment, or connect wirelessly to other instruments. Heavy instruments, such as drumdecks and theremins, are less portable and generally need to be stationary while played. Some instruments have the tech trait.

Hardlight Handwraps (Advanced).name: Hardlight Handwraps (Advanced)
Hardlight Handwraps (Advanced).description:
Upgrades 2

These handwraps can be customized to shine in any color in the visible spectrum when used, and some manufacturers offer custom firmware that modifies their appearance to manifest as a claw, oversized fist, or hammerhead. They can even be worn in alternate ways on other parts of the body. These handwraps enhance your unarmed attacks as if they were manufactured weapons, allowing you to add an upgrade to your unarmed attacks. Treat the handwraps as melee weapons in the brawling group with light Bulk for these purposes.

Your unarmed attacks gain the tracking +1 trait and increase the damage to two dice.

Crystallized Shockshard (Commercial).name: Crystallized Shockshard (Commercial)
Crystallized Shockshard (Commercial).description:
Activate f (concentrate)

Trigger You deal damage to a creature with the weapon.

This electric-blue crystal crackles with energy. When you activate the shockshard, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:15] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 minute. If the target has the tech trait, it instead becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}.

Speed Suspension (Tactical).name: Speed Suspension (Tactical)
Speed Suspension (Tactical).description: High-performance cybernetics replace the joints and tendons in your legs. You gain a +10-foot item bonus to your land Speed.

Shielding Skin (Advanced).name: Shielding Skin (Advanced)
Shielding Skin (Advanced).description: Your skin is biologically engineered to protect your entire body, including your brain, from harmful effects. You gain a +3 item bonus to saving throws.

Laser Pistol.name: Laser Pistol
Laser Pistol.description:
Upgrades: 1

This compact pistol is the most popular sidearm in the Pact Worlds thanks to its reliability, durability, and affordability. Most common laser pistols have a lightweight polycarbon body ending in a long, slim barrel.

Breaching Gun.name: Breaching Gun
Breaching Gun.description:
Upgrades: 1

This snub-nosed scattergun is commonly used to blast through doors and walls. Smaller than a reaction breacher, this scattergun is infamous for its mighty recoil.

Cloaking Skin (Advanced).name: Cloaking Skin (Advanced)
Cloaking Skin (Advanced).description:
Many of your pores are replaced with magically regulated and directed holographic projectors that synchronize when activated to automatically match your surroundings, rendering you @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible}.

Activate—Cloak 1 (concentrate)

Frequency three times per day

Effect You gain the effects of 4nd-rank @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.XXqE1eY3w3z6xJCB]{Invisibility} for 1 minute or until you spend an action, which has the concentrate trait, to deactivate it.

Animated Intelligence (Tactical).name: Animated Intelligence (Tactical)
Animated Intelligence (Tactical).description:
Magical circuitry generates a virtual animated intelligence with a customizable personality and no free will. Rarely, a virtual intelligence might eventually evolve into a true artificial intelligence with self-awareness and free will, usually with the help of powerful magic or a miracle.

Activate—Autoattack 2 (concentrate, manipulate)

Effect You Release the weapon and it glides through the air, fighting on its own against the last enemy you attacked, or the nearest enemy to it if your target has been defeated. At the end of your turn each round, the weapon can Fly up to its fly Speed of 50 feet, and then can either Fly again or Strike one creature within its range or reach.

The weapon has a space of 5 feet, but it doesn't block or impede enemies attempting to move though that space, nor does it benefit from or provide flanking. The animated intelligence is considered a technological creature for the purpose of effects that can target it. The weapon can't move through an enemy's space. The weapon can't use reactions, and its Fly actions don't trigger reactions.

While it's activated, the animated intelligence Strikes with an attack modifier of +27 plus its item bonus to attack rolls. It uses the weapon's normal damage but has a +0 Strength and Dexterity modifier. The weapon's abilities that automatically trigger on a hit or critical hit still function, but the weapon can't be activated or benefit from any of your abilities while animated.

Each round, when the weapon finishes using its actions, attempt a @Check[flat|dc:5]. On a failure, the activation ends. The weapon falls to the ground and can't Autoattack again for 10 minutes.

Grenade Launcher (Superior).name: Grenade Launcher (Superior)
Grenade Launcher (Superior).description:
Upgrades 2

Activate 2 Area Fire

The rotating barrel of this automatic breechloading launcher holds up to six grenades of any variety with smart loading technology, allowing its wielder to choose a different grenade each time they fire. A grenade launcher is a martial ranged weapon, has a reload of 2, and a range 420 feet.

Hacking Toolkit (Commercial).name: Hacking Toolkit (Commercial)
Hacking Toolkit (Commercial).description: A hacking toolkit is required for Computers checks to Hack computers. You can use a hacking toolkit to access a computer without using a user interface, but this requires physical contact with the computer or contact via an infosphere or a similar linked network.

Plasma Doshko.name: Plasma Doshko
Plasma Doshko.description:
Upgrades: 1

Unlike a traditional doshko, this poleaxe uses blades of magnetically stabilized plasma that spin to cleave through multiple enemies with ease.

Load Lifter (Commercial).name: Load Lifter (Commercial)
Load Lifter (Commercial).description: This series of support braces allow you to carry and lift beyond your normal capacity. You can carry an amount of Bulk equal to 6 plus your Strength modifier without becoming encumbered, and you can hold and carry a total Bulk up to 11 plus your Strength modifier.

Blockthrower.name: Blockthrower
Blockthrower.description: Salvaged from the set of Sabotage Soufflé (a popular cooking competition), these frosty weapons draw liquid from a tank through hypercooling coils to quickly generate gigantic blocks of ice that can be slung at high velocity toward their targets.

Degradation Grenade (Paragon).name: Degradation Grenade (Paragon)
Degradation Grenade (Paragon).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @Damage[1d8[persistent,acid]] damage

Degradation grenades create violent blasts of magic that rip matter into different realities, leaving behind blackened hunks of matter that dissipate into nothingness. When the grenade explodes, it deals 14d8 acid damage in a @Template[burst|distance:20] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save.

Mobility Enhancer (Tactical).name: Mobility Enhancer (Tactical)
Mobility Enhancer (Tactical).description: Automated braces help you to move faster in bulky armor. Reduce your armor's penalty to Speeds by 10 feet.

Degradation Grenade (Elite).name: Degradation Grenade (Elite)
Degradation Grenade (Elite).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @Damage[1d6[persistent,acid]] damage

Degradation grenades create violent blasts of magic that rip matter into different realities, leaving behind blackened hunks of matter that dissipate into nothingness. When the grenade explodes, it deals 10d8 acid damage in a @Template[burst|distance:15] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save.

Telebond Band.name: Telebond Band
Telebond Band.description:
This subtle silvery band allows for long distance telepathic communication and was popular on worlds before the widespread adoption of mobile telecommunication technology. The wearer of a telebond band can telepathically communicate with any other creatures wearing a paired telebond band within a planetary range, even if the creatures do not share a language. This doesn't let two characters understand codes, language couched in metaphor, and the like (subject to GM discretion). Pairing or unpairing two or more telebond bands requires looping the two telebond bands together for 1 minute. Up to six telebond bands can be in a paired group with one another; attempting to pair a seventh telebond band fails.

Activate—Fact Check r (concentrate)

Frequency once per day

Trigger You received information from a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} check

Effect With a thought, you instantly share the knowledge you learned with the required Recall Knowledge check (even if you learned false information) with any other creature wearing a telebond band that's paired with yours, as long as they're within planetary range. Each creature who has not used Fact Check today can use a reaction to Recall Knowledge and Fact Check.

Glarecaster.name: Glarecaster
Glarecaster.description: Mimicking the glare of sunlight on ice, a glarecaster creates a blinding flash when fired. On a critical hit, the target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Jump Jets.name: Jump Jets
Jump Jets.description:
Boosters attached to your armor supercharge your leap.

Activate—Jump 1 (manipulate)

Frequency once per round

Effect You activate the jump jets to get a quick boost. You Fly up to 20 feet with a maximum height of 10 feet, or you Fly up to 20 feet straight up. You must land at the end of this movement or fall. Jump jets can't lift you if you're encumbered.

Grenade Launcher (Commercial).name: Grenade Launcher (Commercial)
Grenade Launcher (Commercial).description:
Activate 2 Area Fire

The rotating barrel of this automatic breechloading launcher holds up to six grenades of any variety with smart loading technology, allowing its wielder to choose a different grenade each time they fire. A grenade launcher is a martial ranged weapon, has a reload of 2, and a range 280 feet.

Filtered Rebreather (Tactical).name: Filtered Rebreather (Tactical)
Filtered Rebreather (Tactical).description:
This armor upgrade supplements your armor's environmental protections and filters out common contaminants, including protection from most toxic atmospheres.

You gain a +1 status bonus to Fortitude saves against inhaled effects.

Medkit (Commercial).name: Medkit (Commercial)
Medkit (Commercial).description: These contain analgesics, bandages, sterile gauze, and suturing tools necessary for Medicine checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.MHLuKy4nQO2Z4Am1]{Administer First Aid}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.TC7OcDa7JlWbqMaN]{Treat Disease}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.KjoCEEmPGTeFE4hh]{Treat Poison}, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds}.

Electromag Grenade (Ultimate).name: Electromag Grenade (Ultimate)
Electromag Grenade (Ultimate).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}

These grenades discharge a shocking field that deals 12d8 electricity damage in a @Template[burst|distance:20] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save. If you have access to the critical specialization effect of grenades, tech creatures and unattended tech items that critically fail their saving throw against electromag grenades become glitching.

Spell Chip (9th-Rank Spell).name: Spell Chip (9th-Rank Spell)
Spell Chip (9th-Rank Spell).description:
Usage installed in computer or comm unit;

These chips can be installed in comm units and other computers to allow the device to cast spells. Each device can have only one spell chip. Casting a spell from a spell chip requires holding the computer or comm unit in one hand and activating it with a Cast a Spell activity using the normal number of actions for that spell. The spell must appear on your spell list. Because you're the one Casting the Spell, use your spell attack modifier and spell DC. The spell must be of your spellcasting tradition. If the spell has a locus, you must still have that locus to cast the spell from a spell chip. Spell chips can't contain cantrips or rituals. The traits for this item vary based on the spell it contains.

In addition to the charge expended to Cast the Spell, the delicate components that make up the core lattice of a spell chip can be destroyed if used in quick succession. Attempting to use a spell chip more than once per day requires overclocking it. Cast the Spell again, then roll a @Check[flat|dc:10]. On a success, the spell chip is broken. On a failure, the spell chip is destroyed. If anyone tries to overclock a spell chip that's already been overclocked that day, the spell chip is automatically destroyed (even if it's been repaired) and no spell is cast.

Activate Cast a Spell

Frequency once per day, plus overclock

Effect You Cast the Spell at the indicated rank.

Load Lifter (Tactical).name: Load Lifter (Tactical)
Load Lifter (Tactical).description: This series of support braces allow you to carry and lift beyond your normal capacity. You can carry an amount of Bulk equal to 7 plus your Strength modifier without becoming encumbered, and you can hold and carry a total Bulk up to 12 plus your Strength modifier.

Sprayflesh.name: Sprayflesh
Sprayflesh.description:
Activate 1 (manipulate)

A canister sprays neutral biomass into a wound or area of concern (such as a clearly diseased or poisoned section of the body). For 1 hour, or until the creature fully recovers to their maximum Hit Points, you can attempt Medicine checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the target creature even if they're temporarily immune to Treat Wounds.

Hypopen (Advanced).name: Hypopen (Advanced)
Hypopen (Advanced).description:
This tiny cylinder contains specialized nanites that either

  • Remove the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} condition
  • Decrease the value of the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened} condition by 1.
  • Attempt a counteract check against one poison or disease afflicting the target. Use your @Check[medicine] modifier for the counteract modifier and a counteract rank of 3.

Shock Pad.name: Shock Pad
Shock Pad.description:
Upgrades: 1

This flexible nanocarbon circuit can be installed as an upgrade into gloves, kneepads, or similar appendage protectors to emit a powerful electric shock when forcefully applied to a target.

Gluon Crystal (Tactical).name: Gluon Crystal (Tactical)
Gluon Crystal (Tactical).description: A gluon crystal harnesses entropy to cause serious lasting wounds. Your solar weapon or solar flare deals an extra @Damage[1d6[persistent,bleed]] damage. On a critical hit, the target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} until it recovers from the persistent bleed damage.

Pain Journal (Commercial).name: Pain Journal (Commercial)
Pain Journal (Commercial).description:
Your raw emotions empower the poems and phrases penned in this synthetic leather journal. After spending 1 day writing in your pain journal, select Diplomacy, Intimidation, or Performance. Once a skill is selected for a pain journal, it can't be changed. Reading directly from the journal harnesses your painful memories, projects your emotions, and gives you a +1 item bonus to checks using the selected skill.

Activate—Painful Memory 2 (manipulate)

Frequency once per day

Effect You recite a poem of utter anguish to spread your misery to another creature, casting a 3rd-rank @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.EhN3GjdjXChMdiFk]{Share Pain} (DC 23).

Musical Instrument (Heavy, Tactical).name: Musical Instrument (Heavy, Tactical)
Musical Instrument (Heavy, Tactical).description:
All but the most traditional instruments use modern technology to amplify their sound, provide musical accompaniment, or connect wirelessly to other instruments. Heavy instruments, such as drumdecks and theremins, are less portable and generally need to be stationary while played. Some instruments have the tech trait.

A tactical instrument gives a +1 item bonus to Performance checks using that instrument.

Screamer.name: Screamer
Screamer.description:
Upgrades: 1

Resembling a large directional speaker held in two hands, a screamer produces a deafening boom. At lower volumes, a screamer functions as a portable loudspeaker.

Loudener (Commercial).name: Loudener (Commercial)
Loudener (Commercial).description: This specialized cone is installed over the muzzle or hilt of a weapon to use pneumonic pulses and passive acoustics to increase the volume of its attacks. It deals an extra 1d6 sonic damage. On a critical hit, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:24] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened} for 1 minute (or 1 hour on a critical failure).

Maratan Headache (Superior).name: Maratan Headache (Superior)
Maratan Headache (Superior).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{sickened 2}

Maratan headaches are made from brittle hollow gourds filled with a putrid sludge, dealing 18d8 nonlethal poison damage in a @Template[burst|distance:15] with a @Check[fortitude|against:class|basic] save.

Infiltrator Serum.name: Infiltrator Serum
Infiltrator Serum.description:
Activate 1 (manipulate)

This oily serum grants a +1 item bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.VMozDqMMuK5kpoX4]{Sneak} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.cYdz2grcOcRt4jk6]{Disable a Device} for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment-effects.Item.zXZoUqxzXFisGqmw]{Effect: Infiltrator Serum}

Graviton Crystal (Commercial).name: Graviton Crystal (Commercial)
Graviton Crystal (Commercial).description: A graviton crystal increases the forces of gravity around your solar manifestations, accelerating its impact and possibly trapping foes within its gravity field. While graviton attuned, your solar weapon or solar flare deals an extra @Damage[1d6[untyped]] damage of your solar weapon's or flare's damage type. On a critical hit while you're graviton attuned, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} until the end of your next turn.

Arc Emitter.name: Arc Emitter
Arc Emitter.description:
Upgrades: 1

Composed of two 4-foot-long protracting rails, this weapon shoots arcs of electricity at targets, emitted safely away from its wielder.

Spell Gem (10th-Rank Spell).name: Spell Gem (10th-Rank Spell)
Spell Gem (10th-Rank Spell).description:
These latticed gemstones are suspended in a silicon casing and encoded with the magical script necessary to cast a single, specific spell. Casting a spell from a spell gem requires holding the gem in one hand and activating it with a Cast a Spell activity using the normal number of actions for that spell.
